instances.name: penguin
instances.description: Penguins are birds but cannot fly; their wings are adapted as flippers.

instances.name: ostrich
instances.description: Ostriches are the largest birds and are flightless; they run instead.

instances.name: emu
instances.description: Emus are large flightless birds native to Australia.

instances.name: cassowary
instances.description: Cassowaries are dangerous flightless birds found in New Guinea.

instances.name: kiwi
instances.description: Kiwis are small flightless birds native to New Zealand with vestigial wings.

instances.name: bat
instances.description: Bats are the only flying mammals; their forelimbs form wings.

instances.name: whale
instances.description: Whales are fully aquatic mammals with no functional hind limbs.

instances.name: dolphin
instances.description: Dolphins are cetaceans whose limbs evolved into fins for aquatic locomotion.

instances.name: platypus
instances.description: Platypuses are monotremes: egg-laying mammals, an exception to the mammalian default of live birth.

instances.name: panda
instances.description: Giant pandas are bears that subsist almost entirely on bamboo, making them an exception to the default that bears are carnivores.

instances.name: cuckoo
instances.description: Cuckoos are brood parasites: they lay eggs in other species' nests rather than building their own.

instances.name: lungfish
instances.description: Lungfish with accessory lungs can breathe air, an exception to the fish default of gill respiration. Not all lungfish have this adaptation.

instances.name: axolotl
instances.description: Neotenic axolotls retain larval features permanently and never metamorphose. Not all axolotls are neotenic.

instances.name: vampire_bat
instances.description: Vampire bats that have the hematophagous feeding adaptation feed exclusively on blood, not insects. Not all vampire bats do.

instances.name: seahorse
instances.description: Individual seahorses exhibiting paternal gestation have the male carry and birth the young. Not all seahorses display this.

instances.name: tardigrade
instances.description: Tardigrades (water bears) can enter cryptobiosis, surviving complete desiccation that is lethal to all other animals. They are the exception to the rule that animals require continuous access to water.

instances.name: emancipated_minor
instances.description: A court-emancipated minor has been legally freed from parental control and gains contractual capacity. Non-emancipated minors retain incapacity.

instances.name: statute_of_limitations
instances.description: A statute of limitations bars an otherwise valid legal claim if filed outside the prescribed time window.

instances.name: good_faith_purchaser
instances.description: A bona fide purchaser for value without notice takes title free of prior equitable interests, defeating the default of subject-taking.

instances.name: diplomatic_immunity
instances.description: Accredited diplomats enjoy immunity from the jurisdiction of the host state under the Vienna Convention, defeating the general default.

instances.name: sovereign_immunity
instances.description: Sovereign immunity bars suits against a state without its consent, overriding the default that all legal entities may be sued.

instances.name: force_majeure
instances.description: A force majeure clause excuses non-performance when unforeseeable events (war, natural disaster) make performance impossible.

instances.name: qualified_immunity
instances.description: Qualified immunity shields government officials from civil liability for actions taken in good faith within their official authority.

instances.name: constitutional_supremacy
instances.description: Under the Supremacy Clause, a state law that conflicts with federal law is preempted and unenforceable, defeating the enforceability default.

instances.name: ultra_vires
instances.description: An ultra vires act—one beyond a corporation's legal authority—is void, defeating the default that corporate acts are legally valid.

instances.name: laches
instances.description: The equitable doctrine of laches bars a claim when the plaintiff unreasonably delayed asserting it and the delay prejudiced the defendant, defeating the equity court's default presumption that claims are valid.

instances.name: metallic_glass
instances.description: Metallic glasses (e.g. Vitreloy) are amorphous solids that lack crystalline structure, making them tough rather than brittle.

instances.name: aerogel
instances.description: Silica aerogels are among the least dense solid materials ever made, composed mainly of air, defeating the solids-are-dense default.

instances.name: superconductor
instances.description: Below their critical temperature Tc, superconductors exhibit zero electrical resistance. The same material above Tc still has resistance.

instances.name: graphene
instances.description: Single-layer graphene transmits ~97.7% of light because of its monolayer thickness; multilayer graphene remains opaque.

instances.name: nitinol_sma
instances.description: NiTi alloy in its thermoelastic martensite phase recovers its original shape when heated; annealed NiTi deforms permanently.

instances.name: piezoelectric
instances.description: Piezoelectric crystals like quartz generate electrical charge under mechanical stress, defeating the default passivity of crystals.

instances.name: thermoelectric
instances.description: Thermoelectric materials convert a temperature gradient directly into electrical voltage via the Seebeck effect.

instances.name: superhydrophobic
instances.description: Superhydrophobic surfaces (contact angle > 150°) repel water completely, defeating the default wettability of polymers.

instances.name: metamaterial
instances.description: Electromagnetic metamaterials can exhibit negative refractive indices through engineered subwavelength structures, defeating the natural default.
